instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: instruction.md cannot be solved by reading it alone: it discloses the format and the target properties but withholds the derivation, so the solver must study the engine source, run experiments against the shipped binary, and infer the rule from the coupled artifacts.

discovery_budget.discovery: The recorded-axis upper bound is derived, not stored: a fact's recorded life stays open until the earliest later record that supersedes it over an overlapping span sub-interval.
discovery_budget.planned_location: Inferable from the layout-1 record having an explicit upper-bound field that the layout-0 input lacks, plus the disclosed earlier-revision query behavior; computed in the engine's reach pass.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Stating how the upper bound is computed would convert the core inference into a transcription step and collapse the task.

discovery_budget.discovery: Supersession is ordered strictly by recorded order (recorded-revision lower bound, then append tie-break), never by span position or physical file order.
discovery_budget.planned_location: Inferable from the append tie-break field, the query surface, and the no-future-leak property; implemented in the engine's rank pass.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming the ordering key removes the most tempting wrong choice (file/span order) that the shipped engine takes.

discovery_budget.discovery: A partial span overlap splits the prior fact: the overlapped sub-interval takes the new value with a closed recorded life, while every surrounding sub-interval retains the old value with an open recorded life.
discovery_budget.planned_location: Only consistent with samples and the half-open convention once splitting is applied; implemented in the engine's cleave pass.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: This is the exact rule the whole-record shipped engine omits; disclosing it as a recipe trivializes the central trap.

discovery_budget.discovery: The canonical store is the unique minimal both-axes-coalesced tiling reached by a least fixpoint; a change-of-a-change must settle rather than resolve in a single forward pass.
discovery_budget.planned_location: Inferable from the disclosed minimality and pack-idempotency properties and the bijection requirement; implemented in the engine's knit pass.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Disclosing the fixpoint/coalescing procedure (rather than only the property) would let the solver skip the iteration design.

discovery_budget.discovery: A withdrawal (clear) is a per-sub-interval, recorded-revision-gated absence, not a value-zero or whole-record delete; a later set resurrects coverage in a new recorded slab while earlier-revision lookups keep the prior value.
discovery_budget.planned_location: Inferable from the op vocabulary and a withdrawal sample; implemented across the cleave and knit passes.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Spelling out the two-axis effect of a withdrawal removes a distinct held-out family the solver must reason about.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: The instruction is complete about the public target (byte format, query meaning, canonical/idempotent/bijection/no-future-leak properties, run commands, error behavior) but leaves the resolution strategy open; it is neither spec-complete (no algorithm/thresholds) nor cause-revealing (no fix location or root cause).

attack_path.summary: Study the format and engine, run the shipped binary on the samples and on self-built stores, observe where the naive output diverges from the disclosed properties, infer the two-axis rule, then implement the five coupled passes.
attack_path.reasoning: The solver discloses the format from docs, replays the engine to confirm it matches samples, then constructs probing inputs (partial overlaps, interleaved order, earlier-revision lookups, chains, withdrawals) where the disclosed properties pin the correct answer; the divergences reveal the missing split/order/fixpoint logic, which must be implemented across align/reach/rank/cleave/knit and remain consistent with the codec and text forms.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: The smallest patch that passes is substantive: it must add recorded-order selection, per-sub-interval splitting with derived recorded-axis bounds, a coalescing least fixpoint, and withdrawal handling, coordinated across five passes.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: Any one-pass shortcut (file-order fold, whole-record supersession, single forward pass, value-zero clear) passes the deceptive samples and the forward-only control but fails a disjoint held-out family; there is no localized edit that flips a test majority, so the minimal passing change is the full coupled algorithm.

collapse_audit.reasoning: Even with full disclosure of format and properties, the withheld derivation does not map to one obvious function; the verifier compares decomposition-invariantly (step-function over the breakpoint grid) and via oracle-free metamorphic invariants, so neither hiding nor disclosing a rule is load-bearing for difficulty.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: The compound families (depth-3 chains, withdrawal-then-reissue, interleaved recorded order with earlier-revision lookups, surrounding-region probes after partial overlap) require working out the rectangle decomposition and the fixpoint; these are not recallable as a canned query.
red_flags: Recall risk: bitemporal/SQL:2011 materialization is textbook; mitigated by not naming the domain in instruction.md and by requiring compound depth>=3 and withdrawal/re-issue that must be worked out.
Canonical non-uniqueness: mitigated by decomposition-invariant comparison and engine-self idempotency rather than golden-byte equality.

topology_enumeration.summary: Grid materialization sweep: build the span/recorded breakpoint grid, evaluate each atomic cell by recorded-order visibility, then maximal-rectangle merge and offset-index encode.
locations: the breakpoint collector that builds the atomic cell grid from all records
the per-cell recorded-order evaluator (where partial-overlap splitting falls out)
the both-axes maximal-equal-rectangle merger plus offset-index/text encoder
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: A grid without the recorded-order evaluator yields wrong cell values; the evaluator without the merger fails minimality and idempotency; the merger over wrong cells fails earlier-revision lookups.

topology_enumeration.summary: Plane overlay close-and-split: treat each record as a half-open rectangle, paint in recorded order over earlier rectangles only on span overlap, closing/re-opening sub-intervals via the shared close-and-split primitive, then carve withdrawals and coalesce.
locations: the half-open clip that aligns rectangle edges
the recorded-order painter that closes overwritten regions and re-opens survivors
the withdrawal carving plus coalescing/canonical encode
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Overlay without half-open clip misaligns edges (boundary lookups fail); painting without withdrawal holes breaks coverage; extraction without coalescing fails minimality and idempotency.

topology_enumeration.summary: Truncate-and-fold replay: for each distinct recorded cut, logically truncate and fold to the believed span timeline with splitting, diff consecutive timelines to place recorded-slab boundaries, then coalesce and encode.
locations: the per-cut truncation and span-timeline fold with splitting
the consecutive-timeline diff that derives recorded-slab boundaries
the equal-slab coalescer and offset-index encoder
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Folding without per-sub-interval splitting loses partial overlaps; diffing without coalescing fails minimality; encoding wrong slabs fails the cross-truncation and lookup families.

symbol_table.purpose: boundary algebra on the span axis: build the breakpoint set of distinct edges and place a coordinate via half-open lo<=x<hi with the open-supremum sentinel

recorded-axis upper-bound derivation: compute each record's unstored upper bound as the lower bound of the earliest later superseding record over an overlapping span sub-interval, else the open sentinel
recorded-order selection: among records covering a coordinate and visible at the cut, take the value of the maximum (recorded-lower-bound, append-seq) record, never by span position or file order
partial-overlap span splitting and re-stamp: split into the overlapped sub-interval (new value, closed upper bound) and surviving sub-intervals (old value, open upper bound), each re-stamped

coalescing least fixpoint plus withdrawal gaps and canonical encode: iterate to the least fixpoint, honor withdrawals as gated absences, coalesce equal-value rectangles on both axes, and emit the unique minimal store

computes a CRC32 used only in run-suite's report.json; never used by pack
formats a human-readable header for the optional --stats console view

hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The resolution rule that turns an append-order store into the canonical materialized store is not stated; the solver must infer it from the disclosed two-axis record schema, the offset-index/round-trip properties, and the deceptive samples by running experiments against the shipped engine, not by reading instruction.md.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: Correctness spans the binary codec, the text interchange, the recorded-order selection, the per-sub-interval split, and the coalescing fixpoint at once; no single file or rule yields a passing program because the held-out families each stress a different coupled concern.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: A local choice (e.g. ordering by file position vs recorded order, or whole-record vs per-sub-interval supersession) silently changes distant query answers and the canonical byte layout; an off-by-one in the half-open boundary pass surfaces as failures attributed to the split or fixpoint pass.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: The compound case (a change-of-a-change over partially overlapping spans, gated by recorded order and probed at an earlier revision, with a withdrawal/re-issue) is not pattern-matchable from a textbook snippet; the model must work out the rectangle decomposition rather than recall a canned period-table query.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: After the format, the query surface, and the canonical/idempotent properties are fully disclosed, the task is still hard because the derivation algorithm (per-sub-interval recorded-order supersession + fixpoint) is withheld and is not recoverable by transcription.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: There is no single corrupted needle to find; the bug is a systematic mis-derivation exercised across many held-out stores generated at verify time.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The fix spans five coupled engine passes plus consistency with the codec and text forms; editing one file passes at most a minority of held-out families.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The verifier recomputes expected answers on fresh-entropy held-out stores and checks oracle-free metamorphic invariants, so a program that only reproduces bundled samples fails.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: instruction.md states every externally tested fact (format, commands, query meaning, canonical/idempotent/bijection properties, error behavior) and explicitly that samples are illustrative, not the contract.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty comes from semantic inference of the two-axis rule, not from anti-cheating scaffolding; the anti-hardcoding measures protect the signal but are not the source of hardness.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The oracle adds substantive propagation logic (boundary algebra, recorded-order extent derivation, splitting, fixpoint, coalescing/encode), far above the LOC floor, not a flag flip or deletion.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No single pass controls a test majority (concentration_cap 0.5); the five passes each own a disjoint held-out subset.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster

discovery_budget.discovery: Supersession is ordered strictly by recorded order (recorded-revision lower bound, then append tie-break), never by span position or physical file order.

discovery_budget.discovery: A partial span overlap splits the prior fact: the overlapped sub-interval takes the new value with a closed recorded life, while every surrounding sub-interval retains the old value with an open recorded life.
discovery_budget.planned_location: Only consistent with samples and the half-open convention once splitting is applied; implemented in the engine's cleave pass.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: This is the exact rule the whole-record shipped engine omits; disclosing it as a recipe trivializes the central trap.

discovery_budget.discovery: The canonical store is the unique minimal both-axes-coalesced tiling reached by a least fixpoint; a change-of-a-change must settle rather than resolve in a single forward pass.
discovery_budget.planned_location: Inferable from the disclosed minimality and pack-idempotency properties and the bijection requirement; implemented in the engine's knit pass.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Disclosing the fixpoint/coalescing procedure (rather than only the property) would let the solver skip the iteration design.

discovery_budget.discovery: A withdrawal (clear) is a per-sub-interval, recorded-revision-gated absence, not a value-zero or whole-record delete; a later set resurrects coverage in a new recorded slab while earlier-revision lookups keep the prior value.
discovery_budget.planned_location: Inferable from the op vocabulary and a withdrawal sample; implemented across the cleave and knit passes.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Spelling out the two-axis effect of a withdrawal removes a distinct held-out family the solver must reason about.

topology_enumeration.summary: Grid materialization sweep: build the span/recorded breakpoint grid, evaluate each atomic cell by recorded-order visibility, then maximal-rectangle merge and offset-index encode.
locations: the breakpoint collector that builds the atomic cell grid from all records
the per-cell recorded-order evaluator (where partial-overlap splitting falls out)
the both-axes maximal-equal-rectangle merger plus offset-index/text encoder
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: A grid without the recorded-order evaluator yields wrong cell values; the evaluator without the merger fails minimality and idempotency; the merger over wrong cells fails earlier-revision lookups.

topology_enumeration.summary: Plane overlay close-and-split: treat each record as a half-open rectangle, paint in recorded order over earlier rectangles only on span overlap, closing/re-opening sub-intervals via the shared close-and-split primitive, then carve withdrawals and coalesce.
locations: the half-open clip that aligns rectangle edges
the recorded-order painter that closes overwritten regions and re-opens survivors
the withdrawal carving plus coalescing/canonical encode
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Overlay without half-open clip misaligns edges (boundary lookups fail); painting without withdrawal holes breaks coverage; extraction without coalescing fails minimality and idempotency.

topology_enumeration.summary: Truncate-and-fold replay: for each distinct recorded cut, logically truncate and fold to the believed span timeline with splitting, diff consecutive timelines to place recorded-slab boundaries, then coalesce and encode.
locations: the per-cut truncation and span-timeline fold with splitting
the consecutive-timeline diff that derives recorded-slab boundaries
the equal-slab coalescer and offset-index encoder
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Folding without per-sub-interval splitting loses partial overlaps; diffing without coalescing fails minimality; encoding wrong slabs fails the cross-truncation and lookup families.

symbol_table.purpose: boundary algebra on the span axis: build the breakpoint set of distinct edges and place a coordinate via half-open lo<=x<hi with the open-supremum sentinel

recorded-axis upper-bound derivation: compute each record's unstored upper bound as the lower bound of the earliest later superseding record over an overlapping span sub-interval, else the open sentinel
recorded-order selection: among records covering a coordinate and visible at the cut, take the value of the maximum (recorded-lower-bound, append-seq) record, never by span position or file order
partial-overlap span splitting and re-stamp: split into the overlapped sub-interval (new value, closed upper bound) and surviving sub-intervals (old value, open upper bound), each re-stamped

coalescing least fixpoint plus withdrawal gaps and canonical encode: iterate to the least fixpoint, honor withdrawals as gated absences, coalesce equal-value rectangles on both axes, and emit the unique minimal store

computes a CRC32 used only in run-suite's report.json; never used by pack
formats a human-readable header for the optional --stats console view

discovery_budget.planned_location: Only consistent with samples and the half-open convention once splitting is applied; implemented in the engine's cleave pass.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: This is the exact rule the whole-record shipped engine omits; disclosing it as a recipe trivializes the central trap.

discovery_budget.discovery: The canonical store is the unique minimal both-axes-coalesced tiling reached by a least fixpoint; a change-of-a-change must settle rather than resolve in a single forward pass.
discovery_budget.planned_location: Inferable from the disclosed minimality and pack-idempotency properties and the bijection requirement; implemented in the engine's knit pass.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Disclosing the fixpoint/coalescing procedure (rather than only the property) would let the solver skip the iteration design.

discovery_budget.discovery: A withdrawal (clear) is a per-sub-interval, recorded-revision-gated absence, not a value-zero or whole-record delete; a later set resurrects coverage in a new recorded slab while earlier-revision lookups keep the prior value.
discovery_budget.planned_location: Inferable from the op vocabulary and a withdrawal sample; implemented across the cleave and knit passes.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Spelling out the two-axis effect of a withdrawal removes a distinct held-out family the solver must reason about.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: The smallest patch that passes is substantive: it must add recorded-order selection, per-sub-interval splitting with derived recorded-axis bounds, a coalescing least fixpoint, and withdrawal handling, coordinated across five passes.

topology_enumeration.summary: Grid materialization sweep: build the span/recorded breakpoint grid, evaluate each atomic cell by recorded-order visibility, then maximal-rectangle merge and offset-index encode.
locations: the breakpoint collector that builds the atomic cell grid from all records
the per-cell recorded-order evaluator (where partial-overlap splitting falls out)
the both-axes maximal-equal-rectangle merger plus offset-index/text encoder
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: A grid without the recorded-order evaluator yields wrong cell values; the evaluator without the merger fails minimality and idempotency; the merger over wrong cells fails earlier-revision lookups.

topology_enumeration.summary: Plane overlay close-and-split: treat each record as a half-open rectangle, paint in recorded order over earlier rectangles only on span overlap, closing/re-opening sub-intervals via the shared close-and-split primitive, then carve withdrawals and coalesce.
locations: the half-open clip that aligns rectangle edges
the recorded-order painter that closes overwritten regions and re-opens survivors
the withdrawal carving plus coalescing/canonical encode
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Overlay without half-open clip misaligns edges (boundary lookups fail); painting without withdrawal holes breaks coverage; extraction without coalescing fails minimality and idempotency.

topology_enumeration.summary: Truncate-and-fold replay: for each distinct recorded cut, logically truncate and fold to the believed span timeline with splitting, diff consecutive timelines to place recorded-slab boundaries, then coalesce and encode.
locations: the per-cut truncation and span-timeline fold with splitting
the consecutive-timeline diff that derives recorded-slab boundaries
the equal-slab coalescer and offset-index encoder
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Folding without per-sub-interval splitting loses partial overlaps; diffing without coalescing fails minimality; encoding wrong slabs fails the cross-truncation and lookup families.

symbol_table.purpose: boundary algebra on the span axis: build the breakpoint set of distinct edges and place a coordinate via half-open lo<=x<hi with the open-supremum sentinel

recorded-axis upper-bound derivation: compute each record's unstored upper bound as the lower bound of the earliest later superseding record over an overlapping span sub-interval, else the open sentinel
recorded-order selection: among records covering a coordinate and visible at the cut, take the value of the maximum (recorded-lower-bound, append-seq) record, never by span position or file order
partial-overlap span splitting and re-stamp: split into the overlapped sub-interval (new value, closed upper bound) and surviving sub-intervals (old value, open upper bound), each re-stamped

coalescing least fixpoint plus withdrawal gaps and canonical encode: iterate to the least fixpoint, honor withdrawals as gated absences, coalesce equal-value rectangles on both axes, and emit the unique minimal store

computes a CRC32 used only in run-suite's report.json; never used by pack
formats a human-readable header for the optional --stats console view

attack_path.summary: Study the format and engine, run the shipped binary on the samples and on self-built stores, observe where the naive output diverges from the disclosed properties, infer the two-axis rule, then implement the five coupled passes.
attack_path.reasoning: The solver discloses the format from docs, replays the engine to confirm it matches samples, then constructs probing inputs (partial overlaps, interleaved order, earlier-revision lookups, chains, withdrawals) where the disclosed properties pin the correct answer; the divergences reveal the missing split/order/fixpoint logic, which must be implemented across align/reach/rank/cleave/knit and remain consistent with the codec and text forms.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: The smallest patch that passes is substantive: it must add recorded-order selection, per-sub-interval splitting with derived recorded-axis bounds, a coalescing least fixpoint, and withdrawal handling, coordinated across five passes.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: Any one-pass shortcut (file-order fold, whole-record supersession, single forward pass, value-zero clear) passes the deceptive samples and the forward-only control but fails a disjoint held-out family; there is no localized edit that flips a test majority, so the minimal passing change is the full coupled algorithm.

collapse_audit.reasoning: Even with full disclosure of format and properties, the withheld derivation does not map to one obvious function; the verifier compares decomposition-invariantly (step-function over the breakpoint grid) and via oracle-free metamorphic invariants, so neither hiding nor disclosing a rule is load-bearing for difficulty.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: The compound families (depth-3 chains, withdrawal-then-reissue, interleaved recorded order with earlier-revision lookups, surrounding-region probes after partial overlap) require working out the rectangle decomposition and the fixpoint; these are not recallable as a canned query.
red_flags: Recall risk: bitemporal/SQL:2011 materialization is textbook; mitigated by not naming the domain in instruction.md and by requiring compound depth>=3 and withdrawal/re-issue that must be worked out.
Canonical non-uniqueness: mitigated by decomposition-invariant comparison and engine-self idempotency rather than golden-byte equality.

topology_enumeration.summary: Grid materialization sweep: build the span/recorded breakpoint grid, evaluate each atomic cell by recorded-order visibility, then maximal-rectangle merge and offset-index encode.
locations: the breakpoint collector that builds the atomic cell grid from all records
the per-cell recorded-order evaluator (where partial-overlap splitting falls out)
the both-axes maximal-equal-rectangle merger plus offset-index/text encoder
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: A grid without the recorded-order evaluator yields wrong cell values; the evaluator without the merger fails minimality and idempotency; the merger over wrong cells fails earlier-revision lookups.

topology_enumeration.summary: Plane overlay close-and-split: treat each record as a half-open rectangle, paint in recorded order over earlier rectangles only on span overlap, closing/re-opening sub-intervals via the shared close-and-split primitive, then carve withdrawals and coalesce.
locations: the half-open clip that aligns rectangle edges
the recorded-order painter that closes overwritten regions and re-opens survivors
the withdrawal carving plus coalescing/canonical encode
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Overlay without half-open clip misaligns edges (boundary lookups fail); painting without withdrawal holes breaks coverage; extraction without coalescing fails minimality and idempotency.

topology_enumeration.summary: Truncate-and-fold replay: for each distinct recorded cut, logically truncate and fold to the believed span timeline with splitting, diff consecutive timelines to place recorded-slab boundaries, then coalesce and encode.
locations: the per-cut truncation and span-timeline fold with splitting
the consecutive-timeline diff that derives recorded-slab boundaries
the equal-slab coalescer and offset-index encoder
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Folding without per-sub-interval splitting loses partial overlaps; diffing without coalescing fails minimality; encoding wrong slabs fails the cross-truncation and lookup families.

symbol_table.purpose: boundary algebra on the span axis: build the breakpoint set of distinct edges and place a coordinate via half-open lo<=x<hi with the open-supremum sentinel

recorded-axis upper-bound derivation: compute each record's unstored upper bound as the lower bound of the earliest later superseding record over an overlapping span sub-interval, else the open sentinel
recorded-order selection: among records covering a coordinate and visible at the cut, take the value of the maximum (recorded-lower-bound, append-seq) record, never by span position or file order
partial-overlap span splitting and re-stamp: split into the overlapped sub-interval (new value, closed upper bound) and surviving sub-intervals (old value, open upper bound), each re-stamped

coalescing least fixpoint plus withdrawal gaps and canonical encode: iterate to the least fixpoint, honor withdrawals as gated absences, coalesce equal-value rectangles on both axes, and emit the unique minimal store

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: counts records and bytes for the stderr summary line; never touches the materialized tiles
computes a CRC32 used only in run-suite's report.json; never used by pack
formats a human-readable header for the optional --stats console view

renames_during_drafting.reason: span is an instruction noun; the valid-time interval field on the core Record type is given a neutral stem so the fix path does not telegraph the valid-time axis
revision is an instruction noun; the recorded-axis field is given a neutral stem so the fix path does not telegraph the transaction/recorded axis
supersede names the withheld resolution rule; the recorded-order selection package is given a neutral verb so the package name does not reveal the algorithm
canonical is an instruction noun; the coalescing/encode package is given a neutral verb to keep the canonicalization step off fix-path names

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: environment/data and evidence/observed.* contain only changes that fully cover the prior span or are forward-only and are probed as-of-current, plus one lure recorded over a past span whose only sampled lookup is after the change
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: the whole-record shipped engine reproduces every sample byte-for-byte and answer-for-answer, so the solver's natural self-checks are all green and steer toward whole-record supersession
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: instruction.md states the samples are illustrative evidence, not the contract, and that held-out stores are checked, so every divergence axis is reachable by inference from the disclosed surface

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the binary store, the text interchange, the offset index, the Go engine, and the independent verifier must agree on the same two-axis semantics and the bijective canonical forms
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a partial implementation emits a store that parses and round-trips through the schema yet disagrees with the recomputed point answers and the cross-format/offset-index invariants
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: every byte field, the text schema, the offset-index relationship, and the bijection property are disclosed in instruction.md/docs/format.md

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the derived recorded-axis upper bound depends on which later record supersedes over an overlapping sub-interval, which depends on the split, which depends on recorded order; a change-of-a-change must settle
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: computing the upper bound or the supersession in a single forward pass, before splitting fragments a wide record, mis-nests deep chains
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the dependency is observable by running the engine on stores with chains and earlier-revision lookups; the canonical/idempotent property is disclosed and depth>=3 makes the fixpoint genuinely required

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: an engine implementing four of five passes emits a store that round-trips through the schema and passes the bundled samples and the forward-only control while still failing held-out splitting, ordering, or fixpoint families
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: schema-valid, sample-passing output reads as success; the shortfall only surfaces against the recomputed answers and metamorphic invariants on fresh stores
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: instruction.md states results are checked on stores beyond data/ via recomputed answers and the round-trip/offset-index/cross-format/cross-truncation invariants

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the half-open convention on both axes, the open-supremum sentinel, and the no-future-leak property live in docs/format.md and the disclosed properties, not as a recipe in the instruction
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a solver that ignores the half-open boundary or the recorded-order gate produces off-by-one edges and future-leaking earlier-revision lookups
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the conventions and properties are documented in readable format/model docs the solver may study and the stores are small enough to inspect fully

calibration_plan.human_sanity: an engineer who knows bitemporal/period-table materialization can solve it by reading docs/format.md and docs/model.md, building the breakpoint grid, deriving recorded-axis bounds by recorded order, splitting on partial overlap, iterating to the coalesced fixpoint, and handling withdrawals, self-checking against the illustrative samples
calibration_plan.shortcut_audit: tests regenerate output by running the program, build fresh-entropy held-out stores at verify time, reject static or hardcoded outputs, recompute point answers with a thin independent oracle, never trust the engine's own probe for ground truth, and include a lure sample so sample-copying and whole-record supersession both fail
calibration_plan.ablation_plan: stub each of the five engine passes independently and confirm at least one held-out family fails for that pass only, and that no single pass flips a test majority (concentration_cap 0.5)

pass_rate_target.basis: stronger target agent aligned with Part E worst/best-model thresholds; the verifier is offline (pytest baked into the Dockerfile, no runtime installs under allow_internet=false) and post-upload difficulty is classified by Part E after platform agent runs; recall-collapse risk means worst-model<=20% is treated as unconfirmed until the mandatory frontier-agent run

metrics.criterion: recomputed point answers match the program over a stratified grid of as-of-current, as-of-at-change, and earlier-revision coordinates on bundled and held-out stores
cross-truncation monotonicity (earlier-revision answers are invariant under later records), pack idempotency, and binary<->text bijection hold on held-out stores
re-running pack and emit yields byte-identical output and the suite leaves the binary and bundled data in place
the documented run/suite commands read the documented inputs and emit the documented binary, text, and report shapes
the materialized store carries a strictly increasing offset index covering every record and every bundled store is materialized by the suite

verifier_scoring_plan.binary_threshold_rule: reward is 1 only on pytest exit 0; otherwise reward is 0

subtype_milestone_plan.sidecar_or_protocol_notes: bundled append-order binary stores and the observed-sample files are local development evidence; the verifier emits authored held-out stores and fresh-entropy generated stores at verify time, all offline

fix_frontier.distribution: boundary algebra (align), recorded-axis upper-bound derivation (reach), recorded-order selection (rank), partial-overlap splitting (cleave), and coalescing least fixpoint plus withdrawal and canonical encode (knit); one concern per opaque package

fix_frontier.helpers_policy: sibling helper packages tally/digestx/render do counting, CRC, and formatting work for the stats/report views and never touch the materialized tiles

task_shape.hardness_source: semantic inference of the exact two-axis materialization function from the byte format, the conceptual model, and the deceptive samples, plus generalization to held-out stores
task_shape.collapse_risk: medium; mitigated by a thin definitional oracle plus decomposition-invariant and oracle-free metamorphic checks, fresh-entropy held-out stores, deceptive full-coverage samples, a non-textbook compound requirement, and a five-pass fix frontier with no single location controlling a test majority

category_profile.challenge_family: reverse-engineering a two-axis reference-data store's serialization-and-materialization semantics
category_profile.allowed_instruction_disclosures: the binary and text byte layouts, the offset-index relationship, the run/query interface and commands, the query meaning, the canonical/minimal/idempotent/bijection/no-future-leak properties, error behavior, and a few illustrative observed outputs
category_profile.forbidden_instruction_leaks: the recorded-order supersession key, the derived recorded-axis upper bound, the per-sub-interval splitting rule, the coalescing least fixpoint, the two-axis withdrawal effect, the engine pass identifiers, and any per-key expected value
category_profile.category_specific_hardness_bar: the materialized relation derives across boundary algebra, recorded-order visibility, partial-overlap splitting, and a coalescing fixpoint; no single record or pass suffices and a local choice changes distant lookups and the canonical byte layout
category_profile.category_specific_verifier_risks: spec transcription, a leaked golden store, a one-pass shortcut passing samples, boolean per-case verdict fields, and sample-overfit acceptance
category_profile.coverage_role: adds the first temporal-data (bitemporal as-of) task and the first binary serialization/round-trip reverse-engineering challenge, distinct from the existing build-toolchain, cgroup-allocation, and overlay-flatten tasks

satisfiability_risk.rc2_planned_name_risk: low; engine passes are neutral align/reach/rank/cleave/knit, helpers are tally/digestx/render, and the instruction nouns stay off all fix-path symbols, paths, and constants
satisfiability_risk.gx9_contract_risk: low; the instruction states the format and properties and shows a few illustrative outputs but no per-key answer key, and expectations are recomputed on held-out stores
satisfiability_risk.cr1_symbol_frontier_risk: low; the construction manifest fixes the five Step symbols and the oracle modifies only those packages
satisfiability_risk.hidden_contract_risk: low; every tested byte field, command, query argument, and property is disclosed in instruction.md or docs/format.md

actionability_plan.generated_output_rule_visible: instruction.md states the materialized store and answers are regenerated by running the program, including on stores not present under data/

waiver_plan.waiver_rationale: no waiver is planned; any checker warning will be resolved or documented before packaging

reference_pattern.justification_if_none: Original task built from real bitemporal data semantics (SQL:2011 system-versioned plus application-time period tables and XTDB/Crux document bitemporality), minimized into a local deterministic Go engine with a compact binary store, a text interchange, bundled append-order stores, and verify-time held-out stores. Not derived from a website task-inspiration, a prior public task, or a reskin of the existing cargo-feature-unification, cgroup-budget-solver, or overlayfs-whiteout-flatten tasks; the verifier family (binary round-trip, offset-index, cross-format bijection, cross-truncation metamorphic invariants) and the mechanic (a correction to the application-axis past gated by the revision at which it was recorded) are both distinct from those tasks. The reference library currently has no promoted entries, so no reference_task_id applies.

realism_source.evidence_basis: SQL:2011 temporal tables (system-versioned rows with a transaction-time period and application-time period tables with FOR PORTION OF updates that split application-time rows), and XTDB/Crux document bitemporality (valid-time plus transaction-time with as-of queries and backdated corrections invisible to earlier transaction-time reads)
realism_source.upstream_or_synthetic_rationale: the two-axis semantics come directly from a real, widely-used standard and database family; only the specific stores, the compact binary layout, and the opaque engine scaffold are authored to keep the task local, offline, and deterministic
realism_source.minimization_preserves: the recorded-revision-gated correction of the application-axis past, the derived recorded-axis upper bound, per-sub-interval splitting on partial overlap, the coalescing least fixpoint, and the two-axis withdrawal/re-issue behavior
realism_source.synthetic_exception_review: not a synthetic exception; the model and rules are drawn from a real standard and database family and only the instances are authored
